session.title: AI Agent Action Provenance: xAPI, MCP, and Government Enablement
session.topic_summary: The session explored AI agent action provenance and identity, comparing xAPI and MCP for AI-to-AI and human-to-AI interactions, and applying these to government and NIST IR 8596 contexts. We designed a practical xAPI profile for AI agent actions, created import-ready statements, Power BI and Grafana starters, and produced a revised NIST comment set plus an annex on provenance and transparency. The dialogue also covered a policy debate synthesis, and produced targeted job application materials, including cover letters and resume alignment.

axes.label: Adoption Ops
axes.description: Ability to design repeatable enablement and feedback loops for AI adoption

axes.label: Standards
axes.description: Depth in xAPI/MCP modeling and interoperable event design

axes.label: Analytics
axes.description: Proficiency in building metrics and dashboards for provenance and usage

axes.label: Governance
axes.description: Application of NIST CSF/AI RMF principles to policy and profiles

axes.label: Content
axes.description: Quality and clarity of enablement assets, templates, and messaging

blooms_progression.label: Remember
blooms_progression.title: Recall of Standards & Terms
blooms_progression.description: Recalled definitions and roles of xAPI, MCP, and NIST CSF/AI RMF terminology throughout the session.

blooms_progression.label: Understand
blooms_progression.title: Conceptual Framing
blooms_progression.description: Explained how xAPI captures activity streams and how MCP orchestrates tools/resources for AI agents.

blooms_progression.label: Apply
blooms_progression.title: Practical Application
blooms_progression.description: Applied xAPI to AI agent provenance by producing import-ready statements, plus Power BI and Grafana starters.

blooms_progression.label: Analyze
blooms_progression.title: Comparative & Policy Analysis
blooms_progression.description: Compared xAPI vs. MCP roles; analyzed NIST IR 8596 and public debates to derive actionable design guidance.

blooms_progression.label: Evaluate
blooms_progression.title: Judgment & Governance
blooms_progression.description: Evaluated trade-offs between structural admissibility and runtime identification; drafted governance recommendations.

blooms_progression.label: Create
blooms_progression.title: Creation of Artifacts
blooms_progression.description: Created an xAPI profile, example datasets, dashboards, NIST annex language, comment entries, and tailored cover letters.

timeline.title: Mapped xAPI vs MCP
timeline.description: Clarified roles: xAPI for logging/provenance; MCP for orchestration and tool/resource access.

timeline.title: Proposed xAPI Profile
timeline.description: Drafted the AI Agent Actions Profile (AIAAP) with verbs, activity types, and extensions.

timeline.title: Generated Import Files
timeline.description: Produced JSON/NDJSON statements and a standards-aligned profile for LRS ingestion.

timeline.title: Built Analytics Starters
timeline.description: Delivered a Power BI M query and a Grafana (Elasticsearch) dashboard JSON for provenance visualization.

timeline.title: NIST IR 8596 Annex
timeline.description: Authored an annex on AI action provenance & transparency mapped to CSF functions.

timeline.title: Comment Form Entries
timeline.description: Compiled new, form-compliant comments targeting PR.AA, GV.OV, GV.SC, ID.AM, and ID.RA.

timeline.title: Debate Synthesis
timeline.description: Reconciled structural admissibility vs. runtime identification via a combined model.

timeline.title: Application Materials
timeline.description: Drafted additional-information blurb and full cover letter aligned to OpenAI government role.

employer_value.title: Government-Ready AI Adoption
employer_value.description: Demonstrated the ability to translate AI capabilities into trusted, role-based enablement and compliance-aligned guidance for public sector teams.

employer_value.title: Interoperable Provenance Design
employer_value.description: Built an actionable xAPI schema and analytics approach that makes AI actions auditable and explainable across systems.

employer_value.title: Measurable Outcomes & Dashboards
employer_value.description: Delivered PBI/Grafana starters and metrics to track adoption, provenance coverage, and governance efficacy.

employer_value.title: Policy-to-Practice Translation
employer_value.description: Converted NIST IR 8596 guidance into form-ready comments and an annex, accelerating institutional alignment.

employer_value.title: Community & Content Engine
employer_value.description: Produced clear playbooks and communications that elevate champions, increase confidence, and scale learning.

meta.notes: Duration estimated from multi-step artifact creation and policy analysis. Scores reflect demonstrated applied design (xAPI schema, dashboards), policy mapping (NIST), and communications outputs; meta-cognition moderate due to limited explicit learner reflection.
